purpose.summary: This program purges expired authorization details from the IMS PAUTSUM0 and PAUTDTL1 segments. It reads authorization summary records and then iterates through the associated detail records, deleting those that have exceeded the expiry period defined by the P-EXPIRY-DAYS parameter. The program also updates authorization summary counts and amounts based on the deleted detail records.
purpose.business_context: This program is used to maintain the authorization database by removing expired authorization records, ensuring that the database remains efficient and contains only relevant data.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing summary information for pending authorizations.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Detail segment, containing details for individual pending authorizations.

inputs.name: SYSIN
inputs.description: Input parameter string containing expiry days (P-EXPIRY-DAYS), checkpoint frequency (P-CHKP-FREQ), checkpoint display frequency (P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ), and debug flag (P-DEBUG-FLAG).

business_rules.description: Authorization details are considered expired if the difference between the current date and the authorization date is greater than or equal to the expiry days specified in the input parameter.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program calculates the difference between the current date (CURRENT-YYDDD) and the authorization date (PA-AUTH-DATE-9C) and compares it to the expiry days (WS-EXPIRY-DAYS).

business_rules.description: When an authorization detail is deleted, the corresponding approved or declined counts and amounts in the authorization summary are updated based on the authorization response code.
business_rules.logic_summary: If the authorization response code (PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE) is '00', the approved count (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-CNT) and amount (PA-APPROVED-AUTH-AMT) are decremented. Otherwise, the declined count (PA-DECLINED-AUTH-CNT) and amount (PA-TRANSACTION-AMT) are decremented.

transforms.transformation_description: Calculates the authorization date by subtracting PA-AUTH-DATE-9C from 99999.
Calculates the difference in days between the current date and the authorization date.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Summary (PAUTSUM0) IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Detail (PAUTDTL1) IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program entry point. It calls CIPAUSMY and CIPAUDTY, but the purpose of these calls is unknown as no further information is available in the provided code snippet.

This paragraph is the main control loop of the program. It first initializes program variables by performing 1000-INITIALIZE. Then, it enters a loop that continues until either an error flag (ERR-FLG-ON) is set or the end of the authorization database (END-OF-AUTHDB) is reached. Inside this loop, it retrieves the next authorization summary record using 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY.  For each summary record, it enters another loop to process authorization detail records using 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL until no more detail records are found (NO-MORE-AUTHS).  Within the inner loop, 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED determines if the detail record is expired, and if so, 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL deletes it. After processing all detail records for a summary, 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY is performed if the approved authorization count is zero.  A checkpoint is taken using 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT if the number of processed summary records exceeds a predefined frequency (P-CHKP-FREQ). Finally, the program displays summary statistics before terminating.

This paragraph initializes program variables and reads input parameters. It accepts the current date and day from the system. It then accepts parameters from SYSIN into PRM-INFO and displays them. It checks if P-EXPIRY-DAYS is numeric; if so, it moves it to WS-EXPIRY-DAYS; otherwise, it defaults WS-EXPIRY-DAYS to 5. It also checks if P-CHKP-FREQ and P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ are spaces, 0, or LOW-VALUES; if so, it defaults them to 5 and 10 respectively. Finally, it checks if P-DEBUG-FLAG is not 'Y'; if so, it defaults it to 'N'.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It serves as a target for the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization summary record from the IMS database. It first checks if the debug flag is on and displays the current summary read count. It then executes a DLI GN (Get Next) command to read the PAUTSUM0 segment into PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY. The DIBSTAT return code is evaluated. If it's spaces, NOT-END-OF-AUTHDB is set to TRUE, the summary read count (WS-NO-SUMRY-READ) and the summary process count (WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT) are incremented, and the account ID (PA-ACCT-ID) is moved to WS-CURR-APP-ID. If DIBSTAT is 'GB', END-OF-AUTHDB is set to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, an error message is displayed, and the program abends by performing 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It serves as a target for the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization detail record from the IMS database. It first checks if the debug flag is on and displays the current detail read count. It then executes a DLI GNP (Get Next within Parent) command to read the PAUTDTL1 segment into PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. The DIBSTAT return code is evaluated. If it's spaces, MORE-AUTHS is set to TRUE, and the detail read count (WS-NO-DTL-READ) is incremented. If DIBSTAT is 'GE' or 'GB', NO-MORE-AUTHS is set to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, an error message is displayed, and the program abends by performing 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It serves as a target for the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.

This paragraph determines if an authorization detail record is expired based on the authorization date and the expiry days. It calculates the authorization date (WS-AUTH-DATE) by subtracting the PA-AUTH-DATE-9C from 99999. It then calculates the difference in days (WS-DAY-DIFF) between the current date (CURRENT-YYDDD) and the calculated authorization date. If the difference is greater than or equal to the expiry days (WS-EXPIRY-DAYS), QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE is set to TRUE. If the authorization response code (PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE) is '00', the approved authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented. Otherwise, the declined authorization count and amount are decremented. If the detail record is not expired, NOT-QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE is set to TRUE.
This paragraph simply contains the EXIT statement, marking the end of the 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED paragraph. It serves as a target for the PERFORM THRU statement in MAIN-PARA.
